title: InvITs industry pushes for easier M&A rules to boost investor interest
summary: India's InvITs sector, managing ₹7 lakh crore in assets, is urging regulators for simpler M&A rules. Industry leaders want change of control guidelines to match those for listed companies. This aims to attract more investors by streamlining acquisition processes. Current rules can make buyers acquire more units than intended.

title: Adani Power surges past Infosys in market capitalisation
summary: Adani Power has surpassed Infosys in market capitalization, becoming the 11th most valuable company on Indian bourses. This shift is driven by Adani Power's 68% stock surge in 2026, while Infosys experienced a nearly 29% decline. Investors favor Adani Power due to rising energy demand, whereas concerns over AI's impact on traditional IT services have affected Infosys.

title: Nifty Midcap 100 scales fresh peak, analysts see more upside ahead

Analysts anticipate this momentum to continue, with potential for 20% gains in the medium term, as midcap and smallcap stocks attract significant investor interest.

title: States under fiscal strain amid high welfare spend: Morgan Stanley
summary: States are facing fiscal pressure as revenue growth slows and welfare spending remains high, leading to elevated deficits. Morgan Stanley reports state fiscal deficits are projected to stay at 3.2% of GSDP through FY27, while the consolidated fiscal deficit is expected to rise to 7.9%.

title: Non-banks increase microfinance share amid muted demand

title: HDFC Bank denies allegations of disguising interest payout

title: Avaada seeks $800 million to refinance Brookfield Asset Management debt

The funds will come from offshore loans and local bonds. This refinancing will replace part of the debt from Brookfield Asset Management. Avaada is expanding its renewable energy and manufacturing businesses.

title: AkzoNobel rejects €12.5 billion Nippon Paint, Sherwin-Williams takeover offer
summary: Dulux paint maker AkzoNobel has rejected a massive takeover offer. Rivals Nippon Paint and Sherwin-Williams proposed a €12.5 billion deal. AkzoNobel stated the offer undervalued its business and lacked certainty. The company board continues to back its planned merger with Axalta. Shareholders will vote on the Axalta deal soon. This rejection sent AkzoNobel shares soaring.

title: IDFC First Bank receives Rs 514.82 crore insurance payout on microfinance defaults, expects full provision write-back
